commands.review_summary:
This PR broadens WSL version parsing in setup preflight to accept localized labels and adds a Korean-output regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main requires the English `WSL version:` label, so the Korean `WSL 버전:` sample would not parse. I did not execute the tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Wait for contributor-visible proof and required validation output before normal maintainer merge review.

target.title: 한국어 윈도우 패치
target.body:
## Summary

- Parse `wsl --version` output when Windows localizes the `version` label.
- Keep the parser anchored to the `WSL` line so it does not accidentally match `WSLg`.
- Add a regression test for Korean Windows output: `WSL 버전: 2.7.3.0`.

## Root cause

The preflight parser normalized NUL/BOM characters but still required the English label `WSL version:`. On non-English Windows installs, `wsl.exe --version` can emit localized labels, for example `WSL 버전: 2.7.3.0`, which caused setup to stop with:

`WSL version output did not include a parseable WSL version.`

## Validation

- Confirmed locally on Korean Windows that the old parser failed on `WSL 버전: 2.7.3.0`.
- Patched the installed setup engine with the same parsing behavior and completed setup end-to-end.
- Added unit test coverage for localized WSL version output.

Note: I could not run `dotnet test` in this local shell because the .NET SDK is not installed/available on PATH.
target.author: jkf87
